Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (cruelty to animals)
Identification.ShortTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (cruelty to animals)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to increase the maximum penalties for animal cruelty offences.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Sections 444 to 447 of the Criminal Code are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every one commits an offence who wilfully

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: kills, maims, wounds, poisons or injures cattle; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: places poison in such a position that it may easily be consumed by cattle.

Subsection.Text: Every one who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable to a fine not exceeding ten thousand dollars or to imprisonment for a term of not more than eighteen months or to both.

Subsection.Text: Every one commits an offence who, wilfully and without lawful excuse,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: kills, maims, wounds, poisons or injures dogs, birds or animals that are not cattle and are kept for a lawful purpose; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: places poison in such a position that it may easily be consumed by dogs, birds or animals that are not cattle and are kept for a lawful purpose.

Subsection.Text: Every one who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable to a fine not exceeding ten thousand dollars or to imprisonment for a term of not more than eighteen months or to both.

Subsection.Text: Every one commits an offence who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: wilfully causes or, being the owner, wilfully permits to be caused unnecessary pain, suffering or injury to an animal or a bird;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any manner encourages, aids or assists at the fighting or baiting of animals or birds;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: wilfully, without reasonable excuse, administers a poisonous or an injurious drug or substance to a domestic animal or bird or an animal or a bird wild by nature that is kept in captivity or, being the owner of such an animal or a bird, wilfully permits a poisonous or an injurious drug or substance to be administered to it;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: promotes, arranges, conducts, assists in, receives money for or takes part in any meeting, competition, exhibition, pastime, practice, display or event at or in the course of which captive birds are liberated by hand, trap, contrivance or any other means for the purpose of being shot when they are liberated; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: being the owner, occupier or person in charge of any premises, permits the premises or any part thereof to be used for a purpose mentioned in paragraph (d).

Subsection.Text: Every one who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable to a fine not exceeding ten thousand dollars or to imprisonment for a term of not more than eighteen months or to both.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Failure to exercise reasonable care as evidence
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of proceedings under paragraph (1)(a), evidence that a person failed to exercise reasonable care or supervision of an animal or a bird thereby causing it pain, suffering or injury is, in the absence of any evidence to the contrary, proof that the pain, suffering or injury was caused or was permitted to be caused wilfully, as the case may be.

For the purpose of proceedings under paragraph (1)(b), evidence that an accused was present at the fighting or baiting of animals or birds is, in the absence of any evidence to the contrary, proof that he or she encouraged, aided or assisted at the fighting or baiting.
Every one commits an offence who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: by wilful neglect causes damage or injury to animals or birds while they are being driven or conveyed; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: being the owner or the person having the custody or control of a domestic animal or a bird or an animal or a bird wild by nature that is in captivity, abandons it in distress or wilfully neglects or fails to provide suitable and adequate food, water, shelter and care for it.

Subsection.Text: Every one who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than two years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable to a fine not exceeding five thousand dollars or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months or to both.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Failure to exercise reasonable care as evidence
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of proceedings under paragraph (1)(a), evidence that a person failed to exercise reasonable care or supervision of an animal or a bird thereby causing it damage or injury is, in the absence of any evidence to the contrary, proof that the damage or injury was caused by wilful neglect.
Every one commits an offence who builds, makes, maintains or keeps a cockpit on premises that he or she owns or occupies, or allows a cockpit to be built, made, maintained or kept on such premises.
Every one who commits an offence under subsection (1) is guilty of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and liable to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable to a fine not exceeding ten thousand dollars or to imprisonment for a term of not more than eighteen months or to both.

Subsection.Text: A peace officer who finds cocks in a cockpit or on premises where a cockpit is located shall seize them and take them before a justice who shall order them to be destroyed.
The court may, in addition to any other sentence that it may impose under subsection 444(2), 445(2), 445.1(2), 446(2) or 447(2),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: make an order prohibiting the accused from owning, having the custody or control of or residing in the same premises as an animal or a bird during any period that the court considers appropriate but, in the case of a second or subsequent offence, for a minimum of five years; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on application of the Attorney General or on its own motion, order that the accused pay to a person or an organization that has taken care of an animal or a bird as a result of the commission of the offence the reasonable costs that the person or organization incurred in respect of the animal or bird, if the costs are readily ascertainable.

Subsection.Text: Every one who contravenes an order made under paragraph (1)(a) is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction.
Sections 740 to 741.2 apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, to orders made under paragraph (1)(b).
